cards.front: `include` (pipe filter)
cards.back: Show only lines matching the pattern. Like `grep`. Ex: `show run | include hostname`

cards.front: Show only lines matching the pattern. Like `grep`. Ex: `show run | include hostname`
cards.back: `include` (pipe filter)

cards.front: `exclude` (pipe filter)
cards.back: Show lines NOT matching. Inverse grep. Ex: `show run | exclude !` hides banner/blank separator lines.

cards.front: Show lines NOT matching. Inverse grep. Ex: `show run | exclude !` hides banner/blank separator lines.
cards.back: `exclude` (pipe filter)

cards.front: `begin` (pipe filter)
cards.back: Start output at the first match; everything BEFORE is skipped. Ex: `show run | begin router ospf`

cards.front: Start output at the first match; everything BEFORE is skipped. Ex: `show run | begin router ospf`
cards.back: `begin` (pipe filter)

cards.front: `section` (pipe filter)
cards.back: Show the WHOLE block under a matching header. Smartest filter. Ex: `show run | section interface` returns each full interface stanza.

cards.front: Show the WHOLE block under a matching header. Smartest filter. Ex: `show run | section interface` returns each full interface stanza.
cards.back: `section` (pipe filter)

cards.front: `append` (pipe filter)
cards.back: Save output to a file (adds to existing). Ex: `show run | append flash:backup.txt`

cards.front: Save output to a file (adds to existing). Ex: `show run | append flash:backup.txt`
cards.back: `append` (pipe filter)

cards.front: `redirect` (pipe filter)
cards.back: Save output to a file, overwriting existing. Ex: `show run | redirect flash:out.txt`

cards.front: Save output to a file, overwriting existing. Ex: `show run | redirect flash:out.txt`
cards.back: `redirect` (pipe filter)

cards.front: `tee` (pipe filter)
cards.back: Write output to BOTH screen and file simultaneously.

cards.front: Write output to BOTH screen and file simultaneously.
cards.back: `tee` (pipe filter)

cards.front: What three pipe filters does the CCNA exam most frequently test?
cards.back: `include`, `exclude`, and `section`. Know all three cold — `section` is the one most people skip.

cards.front: Difference between `include` and `section`?
cards.back: `include` returns ONLY matching lines (one line per hit). `section` returns the entire indented block under a matching header. For configs grouped by stanza (interfaces, routers, ACLs), `section` is far more useful.

cards.front: Pipe filter pattern syntax supports [...]. `^` anchors start of line, `$` anchors end, `.*` is wildcard.
cards.back: regex

cards.front: Quick triage: show only UP interfaces?
cards.back: `show ip interface brief | include up`

cards.front: Quick triage: scan log for errors?
cards.back: `show log | include error` (or `| include %`)

cards.front: Difference between `append` and `redirect`?
cards.back: `append` adds to existing file. `redirect` overwrites it. Both write to file instead of screen.

cards.front: Do pipe filters work on commands other than `show running-config`?
cards.back: Yes — work on virtually any show command (show log, show ip route, show interfaces, show mac-address-table, etc.).
